title: Protocol for Applying the Qualitative Comparative Analysis (QCA) Method in Social Studies
author: Kopczynska, Ewa
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This protocol offers a concise, step-by-step framework for using Qualitative Comparative Analysis (QCA) in social studies—covering case selection, data gathering, calibration, and set-theoretic analysis (consistency and coverage). It emphasizes transparent documentation, robustness checks, and ethical practice to rigorously identify which combinations of conditions drive the scaling of regenerative urban food systems.
steps_raw: This protocol offers a concise, step-by-step framework for using Qualitative Comparative Analysis (QCA) in social studies—covering case selection, data gathering, calibration, and set-theoretic analysis (consistency and coverage). It emphasizes transparent documentation, robustness checks, and ethical practice to rigorously identify which combinations of conditions drive the scaling of regenerative urban food systems.
